crisis: Displacement from Venezuela to Trinidad and Tobago

tavily.answer: Venezuela has reported a significant environmental impact from an alleged oil spill originating from Trinidad and Tobago. The Venezuelan government claims that the spill has caused serious damage to the coastlines of at least two of its states and the Gulf of Paria, threatening mangroves, wetlands, and the region's environmental balance. The extent of the spill and its impact are disputed by Trinidad and Tobago, which stated that only 10 barrels were spilled and the spill was contained the same day it was detected. The Venezuelan government has requested international attention and possible compensation for the environmental damage.
